task_name: Idaho Weather Stations Coverage Gap Analysis
notes: Exceptional execution. Agent correctly identified 43/44 counties with Payette as the sole gap, accurately documented 5 stations with blank county data (all NRCS), provided precise station counts per county, and created a comprehensive elevation band analysis showing concentration in 4,000–6,000 ft range with sparse coverage below 2,000 ft and above 8,500 ft. Agency comparison clearly articulated the NWS-NRCS geographic split (mean elevations 3,993 ft vs 6,594 ft) with specific insights into mission differences. Recommendations were prioritized, location-specific, and directly tied to identified gaps (Payette County, Kootenai low-density, low-elevation corridor, high-alpine sparse coverage, blank-county data fixes). Report is well-structured, professionally written, includes supporting tables/metrics, and demonstrates no factual errors against expected outcomes. Deliverable includes three supporting visualizations and a complete summary table covering all 44 counties.
